info.title: Soroban Block Explorer API
info.description: REST API exposing ledger, transaction, contract, and NFT data for the Soroban block explorer. All list endpoints use cursor-based pagination (see ADR 0008).

get.summary: Liveness probe consumed by AWS Lambda health checks and smoke tests.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response.

parameters.name: filter[code]
parameters.description:
Substring match against `asset_code`; SQL wraps in `%...%`.
Caller MUST NOT pass `%` / `_` literals.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Numeric `assets.id`, contract StrKey (C…, 56 chars), or `code-issuer` composite.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Items per page (1–100, default 20). Page granularity is per
`(contract, transaction, ledger)` appearance — a single appearance
can expand to multiple per-node items in the response, so the
returned `data.len()` may exceed `limit`.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Items per page (1–100, default 20). Page granularity is per
`(contract, transaction, ledger)` appearance — a single appearance
can expand to multiple per-node items in the response, so the
returned `data.len()` may exceed `limit`.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response.

get.summary:
List ledgers ordered by `(closed_at DESC, sequence DESC)` with cursor
pagination.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response.

get.summary:
Get ledger detail by sequence — header + prev/next navigation +
embedded paginated transactions.
get.description:
Two phases, both DB-only:

1. **DB header.** Resolve `:sequence` against `ledgers` + LATERAL
   prev/next computed via `sequence` comparisons on the `ledgers`
   PK (index-only scan, no heap fetch). 404 on miss.
2. **DB transactions.** Keyset-paginated read of the `transactions`
   partition with full equality partition prune
   (`created_at = $closed_at`).

The detail endpoint reuses the standard `?limit=` / `?cursor=` query
parameters to drive embedded transactions pagination. Detail itself
is a single resource, so no naming collision arises — the params
page the embedded list directly.

400.description: Invalid sequence format or pagination parameter

parameters.name: pool_id
parameters.description: Pool ID — 64-char lowercase hex (BYTEA) per ADR 0024.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response.

get.description:
Reads the canonical single-statement network-stats query (latest
ledger row + `ledgers` 60s aggregate for TPS + `pg_class.reltuples`
estimates for accounts / contracts) and caches the assembled
response for 30s in process memory. See the task 0045 spec and
`docs/architecture/database-schema/endpoint-queries/01_get_network_stats.sql`
for the full data-source mapping.

get.summary: List transactions with optional filters and cursor-based pagination.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response.

parameters.name: filter[contract_id]
parameters.description: Filter by contract StrKey (C…) that appears in an operation.

get.summary: Get a single transaction by hash.
get.description:
Returns the wrapped E3 response from task 0150: the DB-sourced
`TransactionDetailLight` (flattened to the top level) plus a `heavy` block
carrying every XDR-sourced field — memo, result_code, signatures, fee-bump
source, envelope/result XDR, contract + diagnostic events, per-operation
decoded details, and the nested `operation_tree`. `heavy_fields_status` is
`"ok"` when the public-archive fetch succeeded and `"unavailable"` when it
failed (graceful degradation per ADR 0029 — the light slice is always
returned). Per ADR 0033 there is no separate "advanced" view; the wrapper
always carries the full heavy payload when available.

parameters.name: hash
parameters.description: Transaction hash (64-char hex; uppercase or lowercase accepted, normalised server-side)

allOf.description:
Asset row returned by list and detail. Surfaces both the decoded
`asset_type_name` (SQL `token_asset_type_name()`) and the raw `asset_type`
SMALLINT — canonical SQL `08_get_assets_list.sql` projection.

asset_type_name.description: `native | classic_credit | sac | soroban`. `null` only on schema drift.

holder_count.description: May be `null` / stale until task 0135 ships.

AssetDetailResponse.description:
Detail response. `description` / `home_page` always `null` until task
0164 wires the per-entity S3 hydration (ADR 0037 §342).

AssetItem.description:
Asset row returned by list and detail. Surfaces both the decoded
`asset_type_name` (SQL `token_asset_type_name()`) and the raw `asset_type`
SMALLINT — canonical SQL `08_get_assets_list.sql` projection.

asset_type_name.description: `native | classic_credit | sac | soroban`. `null` only on schema drift.

holder_count.description: May be `null` / stale until task 0135 ships.

AssetTransactionItem.description:
Transaction row for `/assets/:id/transactions`. Pure-DB; mirrors
canonical SQL `10_get_assets_transactions.sql`.

operation_types.description: Distinct `op_type_name(...)` labels for every op in the tx, sorted asc.

stats_window.description: Echoed window label (e.g. `"7 days"`) so the UI can label "last N days".

E3HeavyFields.description:
E3 (`GET /transactions/:hash`) — fields sourced from XDR parse.

Returned as the `heavy` block inside `E3Response<TransactionDetailLight>`.
On upstream fetch failure the caller substitutes `None` and
`merge_e3_response` sets `heavy_fields_status = "unavailable"`.

contract_events.description:
Non-diagnostic Soroban events (contract + system) with full topic
array + decoded data payload.

diagnostic_events.description: Diagnostic events emitted during Soroban invocation.

fee_bump_source.description: Fee-bump envelope `feeSource` StrKey when the outer tx is a fee-bump.

memo.description: Memo payload rendered as string (hex for hash/return, decimal for id).

operation_tree.description:
Nested Soroban invocation tree, derived from `result_meta_xdr` at
extraction time (the raw `result_meta_xdr` itself is intentionally
not surfaced — see 0046 spec "result_meta_xdr is NOT returned").

operations.description: Operations with full XDR-decoded details (type-specific JSON).

result_code.description:
Transaction result code (e.g. `"txSuccess"`, `"txFailed"`).
`None` only when the transaction had a parse error.

allOf.description:
DB-sourced light slice for the transaction detail endpoint.

Composed with `E3HeavyFields` via `merge_e3_response` (from task 0150)
into the wrapped E3 response. All XDR-sourced fields (memo, result_code,
signatures, events, operations details, envelope_xdr/result_xdr,
operation_tree) live in the `heavy` block — see `E3Response`.

operations.description:
Operations as known to the DB — type tag + contract_id only. XDR-decoded
per-op detail (function name, raw parameters) lives in
`heavy.operations[]`.

parse_error.description: `true` when the XDR parser encountered an error for this transaction.

E3Response_TransactionDetailLight.description:
Merged E3 response: DB light fields + optional XDR heavy fields.

`heavy_fields_status` = `Ok` when `heavy` is `Some`, `Unavailable` when
the public-archive fetch failed and the caller degraded gracefully.

ErrorEnvelope.description:
Canonical error response body.

Intentionally simpler than RFC 7807 (`problem+json`) — see ADR 0008.
Machine-readable `code` drives client behaviour; `message` is a
human-friendly description; `details` carries optional structured
context (field-level validation errors, etc.).

details.description: Optional structured details. Shape is error-specific.

EventItem.description:
One row per event — an appearance with `amount > 1` expands to that
many rows (per-tx fields repeated, per-event fields unique).

HealthResponse.description: Response body for the liveness probe.

status.description: Always `"ok"` when the service is reachable.

HeavyFieldsStatus.description: Indicates whether the XDR-sourced fields were loaded successfully.

InterfaceResponse.description:
`interface_metadata` is `null` for SAC / pre-upload / stub rows;
stubs (task 0153) are filtered at the SQL layer so they don't leak.

amount.description: Folded invocation-tree node count for this appearance.

LedgerDetailResponse.description:
Detail response for `GET /v1/ledgers/:sequence`.

Header columns are the same shape as `LedgerListItem`. `prev_sequence` /
`next_sequence` identify the adjacent indexed ledgers in `sequence`
order (computed via LATERAL on the `ledgers` PK) and double as a
chain-position signal — when `next_sequence` is null the ledger is the
chain head, which drives the short-TTL Cache-Control branch in the
handler. `transactions` is the embedded paginated list of
`TransactionListItem` rows, served DB-only (memo and other heavy
fields belong to the transaction detail endpoint, not list rows).

next_sequence.description:
Sequence number of the ledger that closed immediately after this
one. `null` at the chain head (this is the most recent ledger we
have indexed).

prev_sequence.description:
Sequence number of the ledger that closed immediately before this
one. `null` at the chain tail (no earlier ledger persisted).

LedgerListItem.description:
Slim ledger row returned in the list endpoint and reused inside the
detail response as the header block. Doubles as the `sqlx::FromRow`
target for `fetch_list` — the SQL projection aliases match this
struct's field names so no manual mapping is needed.

NetworkStats.description:
Top-level chain overview returned by `GET /v1/network/stats`.

Field naming and semantics match canonical SQL in task 0167
one-for-one. `total_accounts` and `total_contracts` are planner
estimates from `pg_class.reltuples` (refreshed by autovacuum /
ANALYZE), not exact counts. `latest_ledger_closed_at` is `None`
only on a cold-bootstrap cluster where no ledger has been indexed
yet.

`generated_at` is the wall-clock time the underlying SELECT ran on
the DB. Cache hits keep the original value, so frontend can derive
two distinct signals without confusing them with cache age:

* indexer-health lag = `generated_at - latest_ledger_closed_at`
* data staleness ("info from N seconds ago") = `Date.now() - generated_at`

generated_at.description:
Wall-clock time the underlying SELECT ran on the DB (`NOW()` at
fetch time). Preserved across cache hits — frontend uses this to
distinguish indexer-health lag from cache-age staleness.

latest_ledger_closed_at.description:
Close time of the newest ledger in the database. `null` only
when no ledgers are indexed (cold-bootstrap cluster).

latest_ledger_sequence.description:
Sequence of the newest ledger in the database (ordered by
`closed_at DESC`). `0` sentinel indicates an empty cluster (no
ledger 0 exists in Stellar).

total_accounts.description:
Estimated indexed account count from `pg_class.reltuples` for
`public.accounts`.

total_contracts.description:
Estimated indexed Soroban contract count from `pg_class.reltuples`
for `public.soroban_contracts`.

tps_60s.description:
Transactions per second over a 60s rolling window. Computed as
`SUM(ledgers.transaction_count)` divided by the actual span
between MIN/MAX `closed_at` in the window. Stable on partial /
single-ledger windows (NULLIF guards zero-span).

contract_id.description: Contract StrKey (C…) involved in the operation, if applicable.

type.description:
Operation type tag in canonical SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE
(e.g. `"INVOKE_HOST_FUNCTION"`, `"PAYMENT"`) — produced by
`domain::OperationType`'s `Display` impl.

PageInfo.description:
Pagination metadata attached to list responses.

Cursor-based pagination (not offset) — see ADR 0008. Aligns with
Stellar Horizon conventions and produces stable listings even when
the underlying ledger stream advances between requests.

cursor.description:
Opaque cursor identifying the next page, absent when the
client has reached the end of the stream.

has_more.description: `true` when further pages exist, `false` on the final page.

limit.description:
Page size that produced `data`. Echoes the client's requested
limit (clamped server-side).

Paginated_AssetItem.description:
Canonical envelope for paginated list responses.

Generic over the item type `T` so every endpoint can reuse a single
shape. Concrete instantiations (e.g. `Paginated<Transaction>`) are
picked up automatically by utoipa-axum via the handler return type
when M2 endpoint modules are wired in. Unused in M1 — kept as
infrastructure that M2 endpoints will consume.

items.description:
Asset row returned by list and detail. Surfaces both the decoded
`asset_type_name` (SQL `token_asset_type_name()`) and the raw `asset_type`
SMALLINT — canonical SQL `08_get_assets_list.sql` projection.

asset_type_name.description: `native | classic_credit | sac | soroban`. `null` only on schema drift.

holder_count.description: May be `null` / stale until task 0135 ships.

Generic over the item type `T` so every endpoint can reuse a single

items.description:
Transaction row for `/assets/:id/transactions`. Pure-DB; mirrors

operation_types.description: Distinct `op_type_name(...)` labels for every op in the tx, sorted asc.

items.description:
One row per event — an appearance with `amount > 1` expands to that
many rows (per-tx fields repeated, per-event fields unique).

Generic over the item type `T` so every endpoint can reuse a single

Canonical envelope for paginated list responses.

Generic over the item type `T` so every endpoint can reuse a single
shape. Concrete instantiations (e.g. `Paginated<Transaction>`) are
picked up automatically by utoipa-axum via the handler return type

items.description:
Slim ledger row returned in the list endpoint and reused inside the
detail response as the header block. Doubles as the `sqlx::FromRow`
target for `fetch_list` — the SQL projection aliases match this
struct's field names so no manual mapping is needed.

Paginated_ParticipantItem.description:
Canonical envelope for paginated list responses.

Generic over the item type `T` so every endpoint can reuse a single
shape. Concrete instantiations (e.g. `Paginated<Transaction>`) are
picked up automatically by utoipa-axum via the handler return type

items.description:
One participant row returned by the participants list. Shape pinned to
`docs/architecture/database-schema/endpoint-queries/23_get_liquidity_pools_participants.sql`.

first_deposit_ledger.description: Ledger of the first deposit by this account into this pool.

last_updated_ledger.description: Ledger of the most recent change to this position.

share_percentage.description:
Share of the pool, expressed as a decimal-string percentage
(`100 * shares / total_pool_shares`). `None` when the pool has no
snapshot in the freshness window (stale pool); the frontend renders
it as "—" in that case (matches the list-endpoint stale-pool
convention from `18_get_liquidity_pools_list.sql`).

shares.description:
Pool-share balance carried as a decimal string preserving the
underlying `NUMERIC(28,7)` precision (no f64 round-trip).

Paginated_TransactionListItem.description:
Canonical envelope for paginated list responses.

Generic over the item type `T` so every endpoint can reuse a single
shape. Concrete instantiations (e.g. `Paginated<Transaction>`) are
picked up automatically by utoipa-axum via the handler return type
when M2 endpoint modules are wired in. Unused in M1 — kept as
infrastructure that M2 endpoints will consume.

items.description: Slim transaction row returned in the list endpoint.

ParticipantItem.description:
One participant row returned by the participants list. Shape pinned to
`docs/architecture/database-schema/endpoint-queries/23_get_liquidity_pools_participants.sql`.

first_deposit_ledger.description: Ledger of the first deposit by this account into this pool.

last_updated_ledger.description: Ledger of the most recent change to this position.

share_percentage.description:
Share of the pool, expressed as a decimal-string percentage

underlying `NUMERIC(28,7)` precision (no f64 round-trip).

SignatureDto.description: Single signature on a transaction envelope.

TransactionDetailLight.description:
DB-sourced light slice for the transaction detail endpoint.

Composed with `E3HeavyFields` via `merge_e3_response` (from task 0150)
into the wrapped E3 response. All XDR-sourced fields (memo, result_code,
signatures, events, operations details, envelope_xdr/result_xdr,
operation_tree) live in the `heavy` block — see `E3Response`.

operations.description:
Operations as known to the DB — type tag + contract_id only. XDR-decoded
per-op detail (function name, raw parameters) lives in
`heavy.operations[]`.

parse_error.description: `true` when the XDR parser encountered an error for this transaction.

TransactionListItem.description: Slim transaction row returned in the list endpoint.

XdrEventDto.description: Common shape for contract events and diagnostic events.

contract_id.description: Contract address (StrKey) that emitted the event, if any.

application_order.description:
Application order within the transaction (1-based, matches Horizon
`paging_token` convention).
